title: eriogonum vimineum subsp. molestum
intro: Eriogonum vimineum subsp. molestum, also known as California redstem or wild redstem, is a subspecies of the redstem sagebrush (Eriogonum vimineum) that is native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Eriogonum vimineum subsp. molestum include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also sometimes used in salves and topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
